demographics.living_situation: lives at home; adult daughter closely involved and helps keep an eye on him

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I'm calling about my dad. I found a hole in the bottom of his foot today — there was blood on his sock so I took it off and had a look. It's deep and there's yellowish fluid coming out and it smells really bad. The strange thing is he says it doesn't hurt at all and he had no idea it was even there. He's had diabetes for years.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: I only just found it because of the blood on his sock, and I'm scared because it looks deep and he can't feel it at all.

symptom_narrative.onset: found it today — we have no idea how long it's actually been there because he never felt it
symptom_narrative.character: a deep open sore on the sole with yellowish smelly fluid weeping out, and a ring of thick hard skin around it
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: he says it doesn't hurt at all, zero pain, it doesn't bother him one bit
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: on the bottom of his foot, near the ball of the foot under the big toe
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: it's just there all the time
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: we haven't done anything to it yet
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: he's been walking around the house barefoot on it
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: he doesn't seem sick otherwise — no fever that we know of, he's eating and talking normally and seems completely fine in himself
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: he's had trouble with his feet before and they've changed shape over the years
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: he's still walking around normally on it, which is part of what worries me

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: diabetes for about 15 years, on tablets and insulin
past_medical_history.how_managed: metformin and insulin, but honestly he's not great at taking them regularly

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: nerve damage in his feet — he can't feel them properly
his feet have slowly changed shape over the years

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives at home, walks around the house barefoot; daughter helps look after him and does the shopping

objective_data_offered.value: a deep open sore on the sole of the foot, near the ball under the big toe, with yellowish smelly fluid weeping out and a thick rim of hard skin around the edge
the skin around the sore looks a bit red, spreading maybe a centimetre out from the edge

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on an unvalidated home thermometer

objective_data_offered.value: his sugar readings on the home meter have been running high lately
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: qualitative caregiver impression, no logged values

objective_data_offered.value: he looks completely comfortable and well in himself, sitting normally, no shivering or sweating, chatting away
